failure_modes.text: Confirmation bias can turn the first frame into blinders that filter later evidence around the anchor.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge the first intuition with slower, deliberate analysis; actively seek disconfirming evidence before accepting the anchored frame.

failure_modes.text: Substitution bias can make a familiar frame feel like the right one even when the problem is genuinely novel.
failure_modes.mitigation: Diversify perspectives by asking what other disciplines or frameworks might apply; test at least two alternative frames before committing to one.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring can distort risk in complex plans by underestimating disjunctive failure and overestimating conjunctive success.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use systematic stress testing: simulate the range of outcomes rather than anchoring to the base case; ask whether the worst case is bad enough.

premortem_questions.text: What other disciplines or frameworks might apply here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What other disciplines or frameworks might apply here?"

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe to accept this perspective?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What would you have to believe to accept this perspective?"

premortem_questions.text: What is generally true of the world, independent of my perspective, concerning this situation?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What is generally true of the world, independent of my perspective, concerning this situation?"

heuristics.text: Use an initial anchor to orient the problem, but keep it explicitly provisional while better evidence arrives.
heuristics.source_quote: leaders need to set an initial reference point for scope, expectation, or comparison while staying alert to later correction

heuristics.text: Adopt the outside-view anchor when the inside view is doing too much interpretive work.
Mandate dialectic challenge so the first frame meets an explicit antithesis before synthesis.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on the operational distinction between useful orientation anchors and dangerous entrenchment anchors.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor persuasion overlays because the raw source is stronger on corrective discipline than on influence tactics.
open_questions: The first heuristic is normalized from the strengths section rather than from the mitigation table because that is where the beneficial use of anchoring is stated most clearly.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on narratives and cognitive-load-theory as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist section is strong on critical-thinking and outside-view discipline, but some concept names are cleaner than the current canonical target surface.
structured_tensions: The source is tension-real around critical-thinking and scientific-method-style equal evaluation even though it lacks a dedicated structured tension block.
